MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-19-2023 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, IN PART, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, AND OVERRULED, IN PART. DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EJECTMENT IS OVERRULED. EJECTMENT AND UNLAWFUL DETAINER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE NOT THE SAME PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 430.10(C). FURTHERMORE, IN ITS SUPPLEMENT BRIEF, DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE AUTHORITY SUPPORTING THE PROPOSITION THAT ABATEMENT OF A FIRST-FILED ACTION IS PROPER. DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. A REMEDY AT LAW EXISTS-UNLAWFUL DETAINER PROCEEDINGS. (FILARSKY V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 28 CAL.4TH 419, 433.) DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATIONS OF UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING AND SUFFICIENT FACTS. DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNJUST ENRICHMENT, AND FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, ARE OVERRULED. SUFFICIENT FACTS ALLEGED TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED FOR REVIEW. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is DENIED. Second cause of action for declaratory relief does not abridge Plaintiff's right to a jury. Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part, with leave to amend, and OVERRULED, in part.
Demurrer to the First Cause of action for ejectment is overruled. Ejectment and unlawful detainer causes of action are not the same pursuant to CCP section 430.10(c). Furthermore, in its supplement brief, Defendant failed to provide authority supporting the proposition that abatement of a first-filed action is proper.
Demurrer to the second cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained, with leave to amend. A remedy at law exists-unlawful detainer proceedings. (Filarsky v. Superior Court (2002) 28 Cal.4th 419, 433.)
Demurrer to the third cause of action for violations of unfair competition law is overruled. Plaintiff has standing and sufficient facts.
Demurrer to the fourth cause of action for unjust enrichment, and fifth cause of action for injunctive relief, are overruled. Sufficient facts alleged to state a cause of action.
Proposed order submitted for review. Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2023 Continued To May-19-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:
DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to May 19, 2023, to be heard with the Demurrer

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2023 Continued To May-19-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is CONTINUED to May 19, 2023, for supplemental briefing. Defendant to provide a supplemental brief to provide authority supporting the proposition that abatement, pursuant to CCP 430.10(c), of a cause of action in an earlier-filed complaint (Case No. 603950) is proper, and that abatement is not limited to subsequently-filed Complaints (Case No. 671276). Brief not to exceed three pages, due no later than May 2, 2023. Plaintiff's response, not to exceed three pages, due no later than May 9, 2023.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT JOSE TORRES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR, First Amended Complaint on file.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
